spark.title: Direct Romantic
spark.tagline: When you feel it, you move first
spark.body: You express affection openly and like to set the tone. You may prefer clear direction over endless “talking stages.” Your energy together is a big part of what draws people in.

care.title: Care & Consideration
care.tagline: You read the room and match their pace
care.body: You notice tone, timing, and feelings. In conflict you tend to cool down, then talk things through. You want to be someone your partner can rely on.

think.title: Thoughtful Planner
think.tagline: You want meaning and direction before you go deep
think.body: Even with a crush, you pause and picture how things fit. Casual fun is fine, but you keep an eye on long-term fit.

free.title: Free & Easy
free.tagline: You love at your own rhythm
free.body: Close time is great, and solo time is fine too. You tend to treat love more like a shared adventure than a checklist.

blend.title: Blended Style
blend.tagline: Several sides show up evenly. Different moods may bring out different yous.
blend.body: Two or more styles tied for the top. That’s normal—people shift with context and relationship stage.

questions.prompt: In the talking stage, what feels most like you?

options.label: If I like them, I reach out first and warm things up
I match their pace and stay considerate
I sort out my feelings and situation before I move
I don’t rush—I let it unfold with ease

questions.prompt: When you and your partner strongly disagree, you tend to…

options.label: Want to clear the air right there
Check their mood first
Name the issue and talk calmly
Take space so you can both reset
Pick new spots or events for excitement
Prioritize what feels easy for them
Like a rough plan and route in mind
Go with the day’s vibe or improvise

questions.prompt: What gives you the most energy in dating?

options.label: Immersive talks or physical closeness
Feeling cared for and appreciated
Sensing shared values or future fit
The spark when you reunite after your own days

questions.prompt: When the relationship feels a bit flat, you…

options.label: Shake things up with dates or novelty
Talk and reconnect emotionally
Sort out what’s off and reset direction
Reset with solo time

footnote: For fun and self-reflection only—not a clinical assessment.
